linkedin:
If you or someone you know has been involved in an 18-wheeler crash in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, understanding your legal rights immediately can make or break your case.

Truck accident claims are fundamentally different from standard auto accidents. Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) regulations govern commercial carriers, and critical evidence — including black box data, driver logs, and inspection records — can be lost or destroyed within days if a spoliation hold is not issued promptly.

WreckMatch was built specifically to bridge the gap between injured victims and qualified legal representation. Our network includes 800+ participating law firms across Pennsylvania and nationwide, each vetted for experience in complex commercial vehicle litigation.

Here is what victims should do immediately after an 18-wheeler crash in Philadelphia:

• Seek medical attention and document all injuries
• Request a police report and preserve all evidence
• Avoid speaking with insurance adjusters without counsel
• Contact a truck accident attorney as soon as possible

WreckMatch makes that last step simple. Submit one form and get matched with a qualified Philadelphia-area truck accident attorney who understands FMCSA compliance, carrier liability, and Pennsylvania tort law.

Do not wait. Evidence has an expiration date.

Get matched now: https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form

reddit_title: Got hit by an 18-wheeler in Philadelphia — what evidence should I preserve and when do I need to act?
reddit_body:
Truck accident claims in Pennsylvania are significantly more complex than regular car accident cases, and the clock starts ticking immediately. One of the most important things to do right away is send a spoliation hold letter to the trucking company demanding they preserve the vehicle's black box (ECM) data, driver logs, dispatch records, and maintenance history. This data can be overwritten within 30 days or less, and once it is gone, it is gone.

Under FMCSA regulations, commercial carriers are required to maintain specific records, but that does not mean they will hand them over willingly. An experienced truck accident attorney can subpoena those records, identify liable parties beyond just the driver — such as the carrier, cargo loader, or maintenance contractor — and build a much stronger case than you could alone. Pennsylvania also has a two-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but do not let that timeline create a false sense of urgency — the sooner you act, the better your evidence.

If you are trying to find a qualified truck accident attorney in the Philadelphia area without cold-calling firms, wreckmatch.com is a free matching service that connects accident victims with attorneys who handle exactly these types of cases. You fill out one form and they do the legwork of finding someone experienced in Pennsylvania truck accident law. It is worth checking out while you are getting your bearings.
